items.question: I've lived here ___ 2020.
explanations: Correct! '2020' is a starting point, so use 'since'.
'For' needs a length of time (for four years), not a point.
'From' isn't used this way with the present perfect. Use 'since'.
'During' says when inside a period, not the starting point.

items.question: We've been married ___ ten years.
explanations: 'Since' needs a starting point (since 2015), not a period.
Correct! 'Ten years' is a length of time, so use 'for'.
'From' isn't used here. The pattern is 'for' + period.
'By' marks a deadline, not a duration.

items.question: She's been ill ___ Monday.
explanations: 'For' needs a length of time (for three days), not a day.
Correct! 'Monday' is a starting point, so use 'since'.
'During' says when inside a period, not the start.
'By' marks a deadline, not a starting point.

items.question: He waited ___ an hour and then left.
explanations: 'Since' needs a starting point, not a length of time.
Correct! 'An hour' is a period, so use 'for'.
'From' isn't used this way. Use 'for'.
'At' marks a point in time (at three), not a duration.

items.question: I've known them ___ I was a child.
explanations: 'For' needs a period, but 'I was a child' marks when it started.
Correct! 'Since' can be followed by a clause that marks the starting point: 'since I was a child'.
'During' would need a noun phrase, not this clause.
'When' would change the structure; the starting point takes 'since'.

items.question: Correct this: 'I've had this car since five years.'
explanations: Correct! 'Five years' is a length of time, so use 'for five years'.
'During five years' isn't the natural phrase for duration here.
'From five years' isn't correct English.
'By five years' marks a deadline, not a duration.

items.question: They've been studying ___ two hours.
explanations: 'Since' needs a starting point, not a period.
Correct! 'Two hours' is a length of time, so use 'for'.
'From' isn't used this way. Use 'for'.
'At' marks a point in time, not a duration.

items.question: Nothing has changed ___ last summer.
explanations: 'For' needs a period, but 'last summer' marks when it started.
Correct! 'Last summer' is a starting point, so use 'since'.
'During last summer' means within that summer, not up to now.
'By last summer' marks a deadline, not a starting point.

items.question: Rewrite with the same meaning: 'I started this job in 2019 and I'm still here.'
explanations: Correct! Started in 2019 and continuing = present perfect + since (a starting point).
'For 2019' is wrong: 2019 is a point, not a period, and the tense is wrong.
'Worked' (past simple) suggests you don't work there now.
'For 2019' is wrong — 2019 is a starting point, so use 'since'.

items.question: We haven't seen each other ___ ages.
explanations: 'Since' needs a starting point, but 'ages' is a length of time.
Correct! 'Ages' means a long period, so use 'for'.
'From ages' isn't correct English.
'By ages' isn't correct here.
